headlines.title: Key Takeaways From Day 1 of Todd Blanche Confirmation Hearing
headlines.description: Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche, President Donald Trump’s nominee for the permanent role, faced intense bipartisan questioning from lawmakers on Day One of his confirmation hearing. Pressed on his close relationship with Trump, Blanche insisted he is not a "yes man" for the president. NBC’s Hallie Jackson reports for TODAY.

headlines.title: US Military Will Test Troops’ Testosterone Levels, Hegseth Says
headlines.description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth announced Wednesday that the Pentagon will begin testing soldiers 30 and older for testosterone deficiency. The Defense Department declined to say whether there would be estrogen screenings for female service members. NBC’s Gabe Gutierrez reports for TODAY.

headlines.title: Trump's Prime-Time Speech to Address 2020 Election Claims, ICE
headlines.description: President Donald Trump is set to deliver a rare prime-time speech Thursday night where he is expected to focus on election integrity ahead of November’s crucial midterms. He is also set to address immigration enforcement as ICE grapples with two fatal shootings in one week. NBC’s Gabe Gutierrez reports for TODAY.

headlines.title: State Assemblyman Alex Bores speaks on AI and politics
headlines.description: State Assemblyman Alex Bores, a candidate for New York's 12th Congressional District, speaks with NBC News’ Gadi Schwartz about why some top artificial intelligence companies are opposing his candidacy and whether AI should play a role in politics.

headlines.title: Trump announces release of American woman held in Iran since 2024
headlines.description: Jared Genser, a lawyer who has been involved with high-profile cases of U.S. citizens detained in Iran, named the released citizen as his client, Dena Karari.

headlines.title: Judge awards Hunter Biden $1.7 million in defamation suit against former Overstock CEO
headlines.description: Judge Stephen Wilson wrote that former Overstock CEO Patrick Byrne’s comments were “not a product of excusable neglect but rather of coordinated strategy.”

headlines.title: Democrats’ deepening Israel divisions: From the Politics Desk
headlines.description: In today’s edition, our Capitol Hill team dives into the latest disagreement among Democrats over Israel. Plus, Andrea Mitchell examines whether Ukraine can build on the advantages it has developed in the war with Russia.

headlines.title: Nearly half of House Democrats vote to cut off aid to Israel as public opinion shifts
headlines.description: More than 100 Democrats voted to end U.S. assistance. The vote sparked a rare split between the chamber’s top two Democrats: Hakeem Jeffries and Katherine Clark.

headlines.title: Indian billionaire denies U.S. investment pledge influenced DOJ to seek case dismissal
headlines.description: Federal prosecutors sought to drop the case against Gautam Adani in May, a significant reversal in a sweeping bribery and fraud investigation.

headlines.title: U.S. military will test service members’ testosterone levels, Pete Hegseth says
headlines.description: Hegseth said service members ages 30 and older will undergo screenings to ensure they “have the biological foundation required to sustain the fight.”

headlines.title: House passes Trump-backed bill that would make daylight saving time permanent
headlines.description: The legislation, known as the Sunshine Protection Act and backed by President Donald Trump, now heads to the Senate for consideration.

headlines.title: House Republicans push for $95B for Iran war, election measures and farm aid
headlines.description: House Republicans released the text of their third “reconciliation” bill, which they hope to pass without any Democratic votes. It wouldn’t offset any new costs.
